item.dc:creator: grrlinmoon

item.description: A 16-year-old boy is being hailed as a hero after he helped rescue four people when a car drove off a boat launch and into a Mississippi river. The incident happened early Sunday, when the car, which had three teenage girls inside, drove into the Pascagoula River in Moss Point, traveled about 20 feet away from shore and started sinking, Biloxi, Mississippi, ABC affiliate WLOX reported. Corion Evans, 16, says he immediately ran over, took off his shoes and shirt and went into the water when he saw the car sinking and heard the three occupants shouting for help. "I was just like, 'I can't let none of these folks die. They need to get out the water,'" Evans, a Pascagoula High School student, told WLOX. "So, I just started getting them. I wasn't even thinking about nothing else." FULL STORY: https://abcn.ws/3RhZHPO
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.title: Like a boss!
item.description: She was one of a kind for sure. I think I first saw her in "The Poseidon Adventure" when I was a kid. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shelley_Winters EDIT -THANK YOU for the Most Viral my Dudes! One thing to bear in mind my dudes as you think about a reply is this... Remember how you aren't supposed to judge people from the past by today's standards? This would be a good example of that. Shelly rose to fame in the old school Hollywood. You had to either sleep with the director or producer, know someone in the business or be really damn talented like she was. Most women at the time were probably never this bold except for a few. For her to do something like this, it was pretty ballsy to say the least. I posted this because I like someone who isn't afraid to remind people that she has earned the respect that she should have received from the beginning. She's not Tom Hanks but imagine someone asking Tom Hanks for his resume and a headshot. Can you imagine how insulting that would be to him? I can see him doing the same thing. You might disagree and you are entitled to do so but for me, I like her moxie.
item.dc:creator: imflukeskywalker

item.title: AmeriKKKan Cops Break The Neck Of Man In Custody
item.description:
Randy Cox was arrested and was being transported when the New Haven police officer driving slammed on the brakes. Cox who was sitting with his hands handcuffed behind him, with no seatbelt was thrown head first in the front of the van. The impact broke his neck leaving him paralyzed, from this moment on Cox no longer has the use of his arms or his legs.
Cox who suddenly has to deal with the shock and fear of being a quadriplegic is still left defenceless against sadistic sociopaths who grow frustrated with him for his refusal to follow verbal commands.
After Cox repeatedly explains that he believes his neck is broken and that he can’t move his arms or legs, the immobilized victim has shackles placed on him after being physically tossed into a holding cell.
Randy Cox is currently in ICU on a ventilator with a breathing tube.
item.dc:creator: Daywalker27

item.title: Trash to treasure
item.description:
Found this on the curb while walking the dog.
Did I say it was free? It’s free!
Sanded, stained, cleaned and painted hardware. The back was in bad shape so cut a new piece of plywood from scraps I had. Then added the world map (spray mounted to foam core cut to fit for easy removal).
Wife added a nice piece of fabric (mounted to foam core) to the drawer to dress it up a bit.
I think it was hand made by a local guy 60+ years ago. Found some papers of his hidden in the cubby and he wrote his name on the drawer side. Giving it to my daughter-in-law as a gift for finishing her Master’s (nurse practitioner) tomorrow. Edit: Thanks for the nice comments! Was really feeling down lately and this was a nice surprise. Great people here.
item.dc:creator: Chasmann

item.title: Roe V Wades Overturn removes access to life saving medicine, methotrexate.
item.description: The post then links to several organizations trying to help people facing this issue while also more discussion on the issue. I'll link them down below. https://twitter.com/_trashville/status/1543030336266948608?t=F4E4DDHDtk5B_CxILc_0Gw&s=19 https://twitter.com/LupusOrg/status/1543329682770255873?t=ocH9l15_2F7MvvZUM8ej5g&s=19 https://twitter.com/ACRheum/status/1542513802263949315?t=7TVpQjj7lqn9aCoWMhbIeA&s=19 https://twitter.com/babs_zone/status/1543433814940143616?t=Lc2wSTBIfruvDEqf24xYCA&s=19 https://www.everydayhealth.com/rheumatoid-arthritis/women-with-ra-may-have-trouble-accessing-methotrexate-due-to-abortion-restrictions/

item.title: Speaking something into existence
item.description: (This post is more of a mental assertion on my part that i'm going to do it no matter how nervous I feel right now about it. If you'd like to stick around for the short story, welcome). A woman that I have been great friends with these past few months has completely taken up my field of view. Literally. I see nothing and pay attention to nobody else except her, and recent signs that I have been otherwise stupid for ignoring have delayed me doing this. Until now. An evening spent bowling with friends eventually turned into just us for a walk in the park late at night, when literally hours prior I had nearly spilled by feelings right then and there. She's smart, and im certain she knew where our conversations that night were going. But I didn't do it. The next day, a 4th of July fireworks show with friends again. We were somewhat late, and we never regrouped with them. It was just us. Another walk through gardens, our path illuminated only by the soft lights hanging above. It was that night when I dropped her home, that she spoke something to me in Japanese. I was surprised that she was fluent, and I had responded back in Greek "Goodnight" over text when I got home as a way of reciprocating. Apparently the Hellenic alphabet did not work on her phone, but she replied again in Japanese "I can't read it, but thank you. Goodnight". What she sent me next, by the source of several native speakers, translated into "It was a romantic time". And if that isn't a green light, then I don't know. But I am going to do it.
item.dc:creator: Mickey421

item.title: All of my Pokémon cosplays (so far)
item.description:
Roserade - Pokémon Diamond/Pearl/Platinum Cosplay by WakuWakuShop Photo by @Momoiimi Fun fact: the head piece is made out of 4mm EVA foam, I had to use an AR photo from Roserade’s 3D model from Pokémon Go to see how it’s head flower should look Date: October 2021
Shiny Tapu Lele - Pokémon Sun/Moon Cosplay by asksenpaicosplay Photo by Cubster Photography Fun fact: I’ve cosplayed as regular Tapu Lele as well, I decided to do the shiny version on a whim. It’s actually my favorite “casual” cosplay to wear Date: June 2022
Keldeo - Pokémon Black/White Cosplay by WakuWakuShop Photo by @Momoiimi Fun fact: this sword was the first EVA foam prop that I ever made. This cosplay was also the most expensive cosplay that I’ve ever made/worked on, much of the budget was from the shoes lol Date: June 2021
Terrakion - Pokémon Black/White Cosplay by Morrissworld Photo by @Momoiimi Fun fact: it was incredibly hot at this photoshoot, I’ve never been that sweaty just from posing before. Also, note the Choice Band on my arm Date: July 2021
Wooloo - Pokémon Sword/Shield Cosplay by ah_dorkable Photo by dtjaaaam Fun fact: adding prop cranberries to the headpiece and my plush to make them both “Christmas-y” was one of my favorite cosplay additions Date: December 2021
Meowstic-M - Pokémon X/Y Cosplay by me Photo by Larry Fun fact: This is the first cosplay that I made the majority of it myself. The wig and ears were made by my friends Lex and Larcy, but the rest of it was done by me Date: April 2022
Mismagius - Pokémon Diamond/Pearl/Platinum Cosplay by WakuWakuShop Photo by @Momoiimi Fun fact: I made the hat myself, and it’s *huge*. It was the first notable cosplay prop that I made myself and it hurt my soul when someone I’m no longer friends with sat on it and never told me like the coward she is Date: October 2019
Weavile - Pokémon Diamond/Pearl/Platinum Cosplay by WakuWakuShop Photo by @Momoiimi Fun fact: I was so cold during this photoshoot. It makes sense thematically since Weavile is an Ice type Pokémon, but it’s not at all the smartest cosplay decision I’ve ever made Date: October 2021
Shiny Cinderace - Pokémon Sword/Shield Cosplay by WakuWakuShop Photo by cosplayjunco Fun fact: the fact that there seems to be a Scorbunny plush for every every holiday guarantees that I’ll be wearing this cosplay at every holiday related convention
Shiny Bellossom - Pokémon Silver/Gold/Crystal Cosplay by WakuWakuShop Photo by SMBPhotographics Fun fact: Bellossom is my favorite Pokémon and this is easily my favorite cosplay to wear Date: May 2019
item.dc:creator: mashonem

item.title: It’s a Star Wars Dump
item.description:
As a bi woman may I just say, daaaaaaaaaang. MOST VIRAL EDIT: I seriously love all you nerds so much. Thank you for letting me be a part of this community. Also please note: I DID NOT CREATE THE ABOVE MEME, I do see the grammatical error and do not agree with the definition of “older” but hotness must be shared.
He was RIGHT there
Edited to avoid getting in trouble again.
This episode lives rent free in my head
item.dc:creator: LadySt0neheart

item.title: Lake Mead 1983 vs 2021
item.description: Lake Mead 1983 vs 2021 Yeah, let’s keep growing rice and cotton in an arid climate. Should have used the $48B spent on the failed high speed rail, to build desalination plants for SoCal. The vast majority of the water being used from the Colorado River is going to your meat and your produce. It takes a lot of water to raise livestock and a lot of water is used so that we can have produce that is out of season. Cities like Phoenix and Tucson pull from other systems including aquifers. Most of what they pull from the Colorado River goes to AG as well. Literally we are all to blame unless you are not only sourcing your food from your own or a local farm east of the Rockies How Important is Botany for the Future http://www.cosmosup.com/how-important-is-the-botanical-for-the-future/
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007
